Class ImageModifier

java.lang.Object
java.awt.Image
java.awt.image.BufferedImage
ImageModifier
All Implemented Interfaces:
RenderedImage, WritableRenderedImage, Transparency
public class ImageModifier extends BufferedImage
ImageModifier extends BufferedImage to provide advanced image modification functionalities, such as rotation, color transformations, flipping, and masking.
Author:
Błażej Marciniak, Igor Popławski

  • Constructor Details

    • ImageModifier

      public ImageModifier(BufferedImage img)
      Constructs an ImageModifier object by copying the contents of the given BufferedImage.
      Parameters:
      img - the BufferedImage to copy

  • Method Details

    • getImage

      public BufferedImage getImage()
      Returns the current image.
      Returns:
      the BufferedImage object

    • deepCopyImage

      public BufferedImage deepCopyImage()
      Creates a deep copy of the current image, ensuring modifications do not affect the original.
      Returns:
      a deep-copied BufferedImage

    • rotate

      public BufferedImage rotate() throws IllegalArgumentException
      Rotates the image by a user-specified angle.
      Returns:
      the rotated and cropped BufferedImage
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the input angle is invalid

    • copyImg

      public void copyImg(BufferedImage img)
      Copies the contents of a BufferedImage into this image.
      Parameters:
      img - the image to copy

    • greyscale

      public BufferedImage greyscale()
      Converts the image to grayscale.
      Returns:
      a new BufferedImage in grayscale

    • sepia

      public BufferedImage sepia()
      Converts the image to sepia color palette.
      Returns:
      a new BufferedImage in sepia color palette

    • horizontalFlip

      public BufferedImage horizontalFlip()
      Flips the image horizontally
      Returns:
      a new BufferedImage flipped horizontally

    • verticalFlip

      public BufferedImage verticalFlip()
      Flips the image vertically
      Returns:
      a new BufferedImage flipped vertically

    • applyBlackMask

      public BufferedImage applyBlackMask(int width, int height, int x, int y, int maskWidth, int maskHeight)
      Applies a black mask with a transparent rectangle to the current image.
      Parameters:
      width - The width of the mask image.
      height - The height of the mask image.
      x - The x-coordinate of the top-left corner of the transparent rectangle.
      y - The y-coordinate of the top-left corner of the transparent rectangle.
      maskWidth - The width of the transparent rectangle.
      maskHeight - The height of the transparent rectangle.
      Returns:
      A BufferedImage of the current image combined with the mask.
